findings.message: archive_item emits no structured log on the success path. A data-mutation endpoint with no log line produces no audit trail and is invisible to the log aggregator.
findings.suggestion: Obtain the module logger (e.g. `log = get_logger()`) and call `log.info('item_archived', item_id=item_id)` after session.commit() so the mutation is traceable via the correlation ID injected by middleware.

findings.message: The 404 error path raises HTTPException without any log call. The correlation ID is never captured, making this error invisible to log-based alerting and distributed trace search.
findings.suggestion: Add `log.warning('item_not_found', item_id=item_id)` before raising so the correlation ID (propagated by ObservabilityMiddleware) appears in the structured log.

findings.message: session.commit() can raise SQLAlchemy exceptions (IntegrityError, OperationalError, etc.) that propagate as unhandled 500s with no log entry, no item_id, and no correlation ID — the error path is completely dark.
findings.suggestion: Wrap session.commit() in a try/except, log the exception with item_id at error level, and re-raise (or convert to a 500 HTTPException) so the failure is observable and traceable.

findings.message: Re-archiving an already-archived item silently double-prefixes the name (`[archived] [archived] …`) with no metric, log, or trace signal to detect the idempotency violation.
findings.suggestion: Check whether the name already starts with '[archived]' before mutating, and log a warning (with item_id) if called on an already-archived item so the condition is observable.

findings.message: No SLO threshold or PromQL expression is defined for POST /items/{item_id}/archive. The existing SLOs in slo.py cover fleet-wide p99 latency and error_rate_pct but do not break out this new write endpoint, leaving its per-route error budget undefined.
findings.suggestion: Add a route-level SLO entry (or document that the global SLOs subsume it) and add a matching PromQL expression in provisioning.py's SLO_PROMQL dict so archive-specific latency and 4xx/5xx rates can be alerted on separately.

findings.message: No domain-level metric is emitted for archive operations. ObservabilityMiddleware only records generic HTTP counters; archive success/failure rates are not independently visible in the Prometheus/Grafana dashboards.
findings.suggestion: Emit a dedicated counter (e.g. `items_archived_total` with a `success`/`error` label) via MetricsRegistry so archive throughput can be graphed and alerted on independently of the global error_rate_pct SLO.

findings.message: HTTPException is imported inline inside the if-block rather than at module top. This defers the ImportError to the error path at runtime and hides the dependency from static analysis and import-order linters.
findings.suggestion: Move `from fastapi import HTTPException` to the module-level imports alongside the existing `from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, Query` line.
